subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear and logical structure with distinct introduction, body and conclusion
Effective use of headings, numbered sections and a contents page
Conclusion ties back clearly to the investigative question
weaknesses: Minor digressions (vacation anecdote) interrupt the flow
Some tables break across pages reducing readability
Redundant chi-squared procedure descriptions add length
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are small lapses in conciseness and very minor organizational hiccups, the report is highly coherent, well structured and flows smoothly overall. The headings and contents page give it an almost seamless feel, warranting the top mark in Presentation.

strengths: Consistent use of appropriate symbols for variables, parameters and hypotheses
Effective multiple representations including data tables and Desmos graphs
Generally clear exposition linking notation, equations and graphs
weaknesses: A few HTML artefacts and inconsistent subscript formatting
Occasional mis-labelled figures and missing units in tables
Blank entries in one frequency table interrupt clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used accurately in nearly every instance, and the combination of tables, graphs and formulae effectively supports the argument. Minor formatting issues prevent flawless execution but the communication remains clear and consistent throughout, justifying the top band.

strengths: Strong personal motivation drawn from a genuine vacation experience
Independent choice of Indonesian volcanoes and data-selection rules
Initiative in extending a standard Poisson model to policy recommendations
weaknesses: Mathematical methods follow conventional paths with limited novel twist
Some editorial notes (âC+â) break the narrative voice
Could integrate personal reflections more deeply into each mathematical step
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows outstanding evidence of independent thinking and a highly personal approach that drives the investigation. Creativity is evident in model application and policy discussion. These qualities merit the maximum mark for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on data limitations and binning choices
Useful critique of model assumptions (ignoring magnitude, sample size)
Clear linkage between probability results and policy implications
weaknesses: Critical analysis could go deeper (e.g., explore alternative models)
Reliance on a blog source without credibility discussion
Reflection section sometimes reiterates rather than critically probes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is substantial and consistently linked to understanding, offering deep critical analysis of methodology and real-world impact. Though some areas could be explored further, the overall depth and connection to learning justify the highest mark in this criterion.

strengths: Sophisticated use of Poisson and ÏÂ² at HL course level
Correct derivation of the general P(t)=1âe^{âÎ»t} model
Accurate application of critical values and adjustment of degrees of freedom where noted
weaknesses: Minor imprecision in degrees of freedom justification
Inconsistent application of the E_iâ¥5 rule when merging categories
Small arithmetic and notation glitches in some tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is highly relevant and at the correct level, with only minor inaccuracies in parameter estimation and category merging. The derivation of the exponential model and its effective application demonstrate sophistication. These qualities align with band 5, awarding five out of six marks for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting Indonesian volcanoes, devising data-selection rules and deriving a general P(t) model demonstrate the studentâs own thinking beyond textbook examples. However, the mathematical path (Poisson + ÏÂ²) is standard and closely mirrors online sources, so evidence is significant but not outstanding.

The student demonstrates solid conceptual understanding of the Poisson process, hypothesis testing steps and probability law manipulation to obtain a general rule. Misinterpretations are limited; understanding is good throughout but not rigorous or novel enough to be deemed âthorough with rigourâ.

Calculations of Î», expected frequencies, p-values and P(t) appear numerically correct; however, the justification for degrees of freedom and merging categories is occasionally imprecise, and notation glitches persist. The work is therefore âmostly correctâ with minor inaccuracies.

Most material is pertinent to modelling eruptions, but several paragraphs (tourism, actuarial careers, finance definitions) do not advance the mathematics and the narrative is wordier than necessary. Repetition of chi-squared procedure for each volcano also adds length. Hence relevance is generally satisfactory yet not consistently concise.

Variables, parameters and hypotheses are stated with appropriate symbols (Î», Hâ, ÏÂ²). Occasional HTML artefacts (e.g. â<sup>â) and inconsistent subscript formatting show that notation is not flawless, but usage is mostly correct and aids understanding, matching the âmostly appropriateâ descriptor.

Explanations, calculations and justifications are generally clear. Minor inconsistencies (changing degrees of freedom justification, mis-labelled figures) introduce some lapses, yet the overall communication remains coherent and largely consistent, meriting band 3.

Poisson distribution, expected-value calculations, ÏÂ² goodness-of-fit tests and exponential probability model are all directly relevant and at the HL course level. The mathematics is applied effectively to answer the research question, though it is not sophisticated enough for bands 5â6.

The student employs tables for raw data, expected/observed frequencies, probability distributions, as well as Desmos graphs of P(t). These representations complement the text effectively. While helpful, they remain standard and not especially sophisticated, fitting the âeffectiveâ (band 3) level.

The student reflects on data limitations, choice of decade bins, economic constraints of Indonesia and benefits of eruptions. Reflection is integrated in conclusion and reflection sections, indicating meaningful thought, but the depth is moderate rather than substantial.

Using the Poisson model to formulate a general eruption probability function and applying it to risk analysis shows initiative. Creativity is noticeable but methods remain conventional, so the work sits comfortably in the âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ range.

The report follows a clear storyâline: introduction, data collection, testing, model formulation, application, conclusion and reflection. Headings, contents page and sequential numbering make the development easy to follow and arguments build on one another without major jumps. Minor digressions (e.g. lengthy travel anecdote) interrupt flow occasionally, preventing the exemplary fluidity required for the top band.

There is some critique of model drawbacks (ignoring eruption magnitude, small sample size) and discussion of Type I errors. Analysis goes beyond description yet does not reach the rigorous, penetrating evaluation expected for the top band.

Reflections are linked to mathematical findingsâe.g., high P(t) informing evacuation policyâand show that the studentâs understanding of volcanological risk has grown. This connection is clear but not explored in exceptional depth, aligning with band 2.

A structured contents table, labelled tables/figures and distinct sections give the essay a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion format. Transitions between sections are mostly smooth. Some tables break across pages and there is sporadic duplication of references, so the organisation is very good rather than seamless.

The narrative is driven by the studentâs personal vacation experience and humanitarian interest, shaping the exploration choices and discussion of policy implications. This clear personal approach propels the work, satisfying band 2 but not reaching a level that âenhances understandingâ throughout.

annotations.comment: The personal vacation anecdote adds engagement but interrupts flow. Focus more tightly on mathematical motivation to maintain coherence.
The title clearly states the topic but could more specifically reflect the investigative question to guide the reader from the outset.
The contents page is well laid out but entries such as Appendix breaking across pages reduce readability. Ensure each table of contents entry fits on one line.
The heading âMath Internal Assessmentâ lacks context about the student and date. Consider adding a subheading with candidate details for clarity.
The repetition of chi-squared procedure for each volcano, though systematic, adds unnecessary length. Consider summarising common steps.
Derivation of the general rule $$P(t)=1-e^{-\lambda t}$$ is mathematically sound and clearly presented.
Citing a blog for inspiration is fine, but discuss its credibility and compare with peer-reviewed sources for rigour.
The Desmos graphs are effective multiple representations that reinforce the modelâs behaviour over time. Well executed.
Policy recommendations regarding evacuation plans and education are well linked to probability findings; this demonstrates clear personal engagement.
The reflection section acknowledges data limitations and alternative positive volcano impacts; deeper critique on model assumptions would enhance it.
The tag âEvidence of personal interest C+â appears editorial. Remove grading notes and ensure selfâreflection points are integrated logically.
HTML artefacts such as â<sup>â disrupt notation clarity. Clean up lettered superscripts to maintain consistent mathematical formatting.
The definition of the Poisson formula is correct but you could explicitly state the domain: $$x=0,1,2,\dots$$ to reinforce clarity.
In Table 1, include units (eruptions per year, eruptions per 20 years) in column headings to improve multiple representation interpretation.
The degrees of freedom formula omits subtraction for estimated parameters. For a Poisson fit, $$df=\text{categories}-1-1$$; revise justification accordingly.
The observed frequency table is blank (Table 2). Ensure data are fully transcribed to support subsequent calculations.
Merging categories should reference the rule $$E_i\ge5$$ universally and justify choices numerically rather than narratively.
The Excel formula figures (Figure 1 and 2) are helpful but captions should describe inputs and outputs explicitly to enhance clarity.
Presentation of Baturâs probability distribution (Table 8) uses vertical bars within cells. Adopt a standard tabular layout for readability.
When merging Batur categories, note that change in $$df$$ must be stated: $$df=n-1-p$$. You mention $$df=2$$ but should update to $$1$$ and explain.
The critical value adjustment for Raung is correctly noted (3.841) and clearly applied. Excellent attention to changing $$df$$.
The conclusion succinctly ties results back to the driving question and acknowledges practical implications. Strong coherence.

The exploration shows some coherence and logical development
Well organized with clear introduction, body and conclusion
The exploration is coherent and shows clear logical development
The exploration is highly coherent with excellent logical development
Exceptionally well organized with seamless flow between sections
No appropriate mathematical language or notation used
Communication is partially clear with inconsistencies
Limited use of appropriate mathematical language and notation
Communication is mostly clear with some consistency
Some appropriate mathematical language and notation used
Communication is clear and mostly consistent
Mostly appropriate mathematical language and notation throughout
Effective use of multiple mathematical representations
Communication is clear and consistent throughout
Sophisticated use of multiple mathematical representations
Consistent and accurate mathematical language and notation throughout
Clear personal approach that drives the exploration
Highly personal approach that enhances understanding
Some relevant mathematics at appropriate level
Relevant mathematics at course level used
Mathematics is correct with minor errors
Relevant mathematics at course level used effectively
Sophisticated relevant mathematics at course level
Mathematics is correct and shows sophistication
Mathematics is precise and demonstrates sophistication
Sophisticated and rigorous mathematics at course level
